creativeBrief.title: The Minimum Useful Video Factory
creativeBrief.audience: builders moving from prototype automation to a repeatable content system
creativeBrief.objective: Define the smallest pipeline that creates reviewable drafts and playable videos.

script.voiceover:
The Minimum Useful Video Factory. Define the smallest pipeline that creates reviewable drafts and playable videos.

Point 1. A draft package should include script, storyboard, captions, metadata, and QA notes.

Point 2. The render step should consume the package directly, not a separate hand-built file.

Point 3. Validation should check both content completeness and MP4 existence.

What this unlocks. When the MP4 is real, publishing is just the next integration layer.

designBrief.positioning: A practical content operations system that proves draft-to-MP4 quality locally before publishing integrations are added.

metadata.title: The Minimum Useful Video Factory
metadata.description:
Define the smallest pipeline that creates reviewable drafts and playable videos.

Key points:
- A draft package should include script, storyboard, captions, metadata, and QA notes.
- The render step should consume the package directly, not a separate hand-built file.
- Validation should check both content completeness and MP4 existence.

qaChecklist: Script has a hook, supporting points, and close.
Storyboard contains one or more timed scenes.
Captions are present for each scene.
Design brief covers visual, motion, audio, caption, and platform decisions.
Renderer can create a local MP4, thumbnail, and preview frame from this package.
